\compilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. If \langfam{SDNNF}{T} compiled to \langref{nFBDD} with polynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}.

matrix.description:
First, we show no polynomial compilation exists.
\compilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. \nocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. If \langfam{SDNNF}{T} compiled to \langref{nFBDD} with polynomial blowup, this would contradict \nocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. Therefore \nocompilespoly{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}.

Now, we show a quasipolynomial compilation exists.
\compilespoly{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{SDNNF}}{\langref{DNNF}}. \compilesquasi{\langref{DNNF}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. Therefore \compilesquasi{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{SDNNF}}{\langref{DNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{DNNF}}{\langref{NNF}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{NNF}}.

noPolyDescription.description:
\compilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{nOBDD}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. If \langfam{SDNNF}{T} compiled to \langref{nOBDD} with polynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nOBDD}}.

matrix.description:
First, we show no polynomial compilation exists.
\compilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. \nocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{nOBDD}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. If \langfam{SDNNF}{T} compiled to \langref{nOBDD} with polynomial blowup, this would contradict \nocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. Therefore \nocompilespoly{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nOBDD}}.

Now, we show a quasipolynomial compilation exists.
\compilespoly{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. \compilesquasi{\langref{SDNNF}}{\langref{nOBDD}}. Therefore \compilesquasi{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nOBDD}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{DNF}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{OBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{OBDD}}. If \langfam{SDNNF}{T} compiled to \langfam{OBDD}{<} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{OBDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{IP}}{\langref{DNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{DNF}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{IP}}{\langref{CNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{PI}}{\langref{CNF}}. If \langfam{SDNNF}{T} compiled to \langref{PI} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{IP}}{\langref{CNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{PI}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{DNF}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{SDD}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. If \langfam{SDNNF}{T} compiled to \langref{SDD} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{SDD}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{DNF}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{SDD}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. If \langfam{SDNNF}{T} compiled to \langfam{SDD}{T} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{SDD}{T}}.

\langref{SDNNF} is the union of the fixed-vtree classes \langfam{SDNNF}{T}. Hence any \langfam{SDNNF}{T} formula is already an \langref{SDNNF} formula, so the compilation is trivial and polynomial-time. \citep{Pipatsrisawat_2008}

\compilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. If \langfam{SDNNF}{T} compiled to \langref{uFBDD} with polynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{uFBDD}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{DNF}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{uOBDD}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. If \langfam{SDNNF}{T} compiled to \langref{uOBDD} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{uOBDD}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{DNF}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{OBDD}}. If \langfam{SDNNF}{T} compiled to \langref{OBDD} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{OBDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{OBDD}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{DNF}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{TDD}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. If \langfam{SDNNF}{T} compiled to \langfam{TDD}{T} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{TDD}{T}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{DNF}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{TDD}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. If \langfam{SDNNF}{T} compiled to \langref{TDD} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{TDD}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{SDD}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{SDD}{T}}{\langref{FBDD}}. \compilesquasi{\langref{dec-DNNF}}{\langref{FBDD}}. If \langfam{SDNNF}{T} compiled to \langref{dec-DNNF} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{SDD}{T}}{\langref{FBDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{dec-DNNF}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{DNF}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. If \langfam{SDNNF}{T} compiled to \langref{dec-SDNNF} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{DNF}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. If \langfam{SDNNF}{T} compiled to \langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{DNF}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langref{cSDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{cSDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{SDD}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. If \langfam{SDNNF}{T} compiled to \langfam{cSDD}{T} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}.

noPolyDescription.description:
\compilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langref{nOBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{nOBDD}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. If \langfam{SDNNF}{T} compiled to \langfam{nOBDD}{<} with polynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}.

quasiDescription.description: The \langref{SDNNF}-to-\langref{nOBDD} simulation can be oriented as in \citet{TCZ_Forthcoming}: for every fixed source vtree $T$, it chooses one output order $<$ depending only on $T$ and gives a quasipolynomial-size \langfam{nOBDD}{<}.

matrix.description:
First, we show no polynomial compilation exists.
\compilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. \nocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langref{nOBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{nOBDD}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. If \langfam{SDNNF}{T} compiled to \langfam{nOBDD}{<} with polynomial blowup, this would contradict \nocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. Therefore \nocompilespoly{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}.

Now, we show a quasipolynomial compilation exists.
The \langref{SDNNF}-to-\langref{nOBDD} simulation can be oriented as in \citet{TCZ_Forthcoming}: for every fixed source vtree $T$, it chooses one output order $<$ depending only on $T$ and gives a quasipolynomial-size \langfam{nOBDD}{<}.

\compilespoly{\langref{DNF}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. If \langfam{SDNNF}{T} compiled to \langfam{uOBDD}{<} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{OBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{FBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{uFBDD}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{CNF}}. If \langref{uFBDD} compiled to \langref{CNF} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{CNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langref{CNF}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{uFBDD}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{cSDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. If \langref{uFBDD} compiled to \langref{cSDD} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langref{cSDD}}.

Rewrite each decision node labeled $x$ as $(x \wedge D_0) \vee (\neg x \wedge D_1)$, where $D_0, D_1$ are the rewritings of the 0- and 1-successors. The $\wedge$-gates are decomposable, and unambiguity of the \langref{uFBDD} ensures all introduced $\vee$-gates are deterministic, yielding a \langref{d-DNNF} in linear time \citep{Amarilli_2018}.

\compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{uFBDD}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-SDNNF}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. If \langref{uFBDD} compiled to \langref{d-SDNNF} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{uFBDD}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-SDNNF}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. If \langref{uFBDD} compiled to \langfam{d-SDNNF}{T} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{OBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{FBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{uFBDD}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{DNF}}. If \langref{uFBDD} compiled to \langref{DNF} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{DNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langref{DNF}}.
\compilespoly{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langref{d-DNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-DNNF}}{\langref{DNNF}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langref{DNNF}}.

Consider the Boolean functions $\phi_n, \psi_n: \{0,1\}^{n^2}\to\{0,1\}$, which respectively test whether, in an $n\times n$ Boolean matrix, that the number of 1's is odd and there is a row full of 1's, and that the number of 1's is even and there is a column full of 1's. \citet{Bova_2016} and \citet{Wegener_2000} show that an \langref{FBDD} expression for $\phi_n \vee \psi_n$ has necessarily size $2^{\Omega(n^{1/2})}$; but since an \langref{FBDD} of size $O(n^2)$ may test $\phi_n$ or $\psi_n$ separately (shown by \citet[Proposition 3.1]{Amarilli_2018}), a \langref{uFBDD} for $\phi_n\vee \psi_n$ may be obtained by simply adding an $\vee$-gate joining these two \langref{FBDD} expressions, since $\phi_n \wedge \psi_n$ is unsatisfiable.

\compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{OBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{FBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{uFBDD}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{DNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{IP}}{\langref{DNF}}. If \langref{uFBDD} compiled to \langref{IP} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{DNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langref{IP}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{uFBDD}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{OBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{MODS}}{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{OBDD}}. If \langref{uFBDD} compiled to \langref{MODS} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{OBDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langref{MODS}}.
As defined by \citet{Amarilli_2018}, \langref{uFBDD} is the subset of \langref{nFBDD} satisfying unambiguity.

\compilespoly{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langref{d-DNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-DNNF}}{\langref{DNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{DNNF}}{\langref{NNF}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langref{NNF}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{uFBDD}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{nOBDD}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. If \langref{uFBDD} compiled to \langref{nOBDD} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langref{nOBDD}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{uFBDD}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{OBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{OBDD}}. If \langref{uFBDD} compiled to \langfam{OBDD}{<} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{OBDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{OBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{FBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{uFBDD}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{CNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{PI}}{\langref{CNF}}. If \langref{uFBDD} compiled to \langref{PI} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{CNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langref{PI}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{uFBDD}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. If \langref{uFBDD} compiled to \langref{SDD} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langref{SDD}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{uFBDD}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{SDD}{T}}{\langref{SDD}}. If \langref{uFBDD} compiled to \langfam{SDD}{T} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langfam{SDD}{T}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{uFBDD}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. If \langref{uFBDD} compiled to \langref{SDNNF} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langref{SDNNF}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{uFBDD}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. If \langref{uFBDD} compiled to \langfam{SDNNF}{T} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{uFBDD}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{uOBDD}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-SDNNF}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. If \langref{uFBDD} compiled to \langref{uOBDD} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langref{uOBDD}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{uFBDD}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{OBDD}}. If \langref{uFBDD} compiled to \langref{OBDD} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{OBDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langref{OBDD}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{uFBDD}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{TDD}{T}}{\langfam{SDD}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{SDD}{T}}{\langref{SDD}}. If \langref{uFBDD} compiled to \langfam{TDD}{T} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langfam{TDD}{T}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{uFBDD}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{TDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. If \langref{uFBDD} compiled to \langref{TDD} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langref{TDD}}.

ocompilesquasi{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langref{FBDD}}. \compilesquasi{\langref{dec-DNNF}}{\langref{FBDD}}. If \langref{uFBDD} compiled to \langref{dec-DNNF} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langref{FBDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langref{dec-DNNF}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{uFBDD}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{OBDD}}. \compilesquasi{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}{\langref{OBDD}}. If \langref{uFBDD} compiled to \langref{dec-SDNNF} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{OBDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{uFBDD}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{OBDD}}. \compilesquasi{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{OBDD}}. If \langref{uFBDD} compiled to \langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{OBDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{uFBDD}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langref{cSDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{cSDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. If \langref{uFBDD} compiled to \langfam{cSDD}{T} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{uFBDD}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langref{nOBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{nOBDD}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. If \langref{uFBDD} compiled to \langfam{nOBDD}{<} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{uFBDD}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-SDNNF}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. If \langref{uFBDD} compiled to \langfam{uOBDD}{<} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{OBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{uOBDD}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{CNF}}. If \langref{uOBDD} compiled to \langref{CNF} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{CNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{uOBDD}}{\langref{CNF}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langref{uOBDD}}. 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langref{SDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{cSDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. If \langref{uOBDD} compiled to \langref{cSDD} with polynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langref{SDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilespoly{\langref{uOBDD}}{\langref{cSDD}}.
\compilespoly{\langref{uOBDD}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-SDNNF}}{\langref{d-DNNF}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langref{uOBDD}}{\langref{d-DNNF}}.

Construct a right-linear vtree from the variable order, then rewrite each decision node labeled $x$ as $(x \wedge D_0) \vee (\neg x \wedge D_1)$, where $D_0, D_1$ are the rewritings of the 0- and 1-successors. The vtree alignment ensures structuredness, and unambiguity of the \langref{uOBDD} makes all introduced $\vee$-gates deterministic, yielding a \langref{d-SDNNF} in linear time \citep{Amarilli_2018}.

\compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{uOBDD}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{OBDD}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. If \langref{uOBDD} compiled to \langfam{d-SDNNF}{T} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{OBDD}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{uOBDD}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{OBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{uOBDD}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{DNF}}. If \langref{uOBDD} compiled to \langref{DNF} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{DNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{uOBDD}}{\langref{DNF}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{uOBDD}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-SDNNF}}{\langref{d-DNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-DNNF}}{\langref{DNNF}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langref{uOBDD}}{\langref{DNNF}}.

The weighted sum function $WS_n$ has polynomial-size \langref{uOBDD} formulas: \citet{Wegener_2000} gives polynomial-size \langref{nOBDD}{<} formulas for $WS_n$ under every variable ordering $<$, and the pointer-guess construction has at most one accepting guess, so the formula is unambiguous. By \citet{Savicky_Zak_1996}, every \langref{FBDD} for $WS_n$ has size $2^{n-o(n)}$. Hence no quasipolynomial compilation from \langref{uOBDD} to \langref{FBDD} is possible.

\compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{OBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{uOBDD}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{DNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{IP}}{\langref{DNF}}. If \langref{uOBDD} compiled to \langref{IP} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{DNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{uOBDD}}{\langref{IP}}.

ocompilesquasi{\langref{uOBDD}}{\langref{OBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{MODS}}{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{OBDD}}. If \langref{uOBDD} compiled to \langref{MODS} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{uOBDD}}{\langref{OBDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{uOBDD}}{\langref{MODS}}.
\compilespoly{\langref{uOBDD}}{\langref{nOBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{nOBDD}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langref{uOBDD}}{\langref{nFBDD}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{uOBDD}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-SDNNF}}{\langref{d-DNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-DNNF}}{\langref{DNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{DNNF}}{\langref{NNF}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langref{uOBDD}}{\langref{NNF}}.
As defined by \citet{Amarilli_2018}, \langref{uOBDD} is the subset of \langref{nOBDD} satisfying unambiguity.

ocompilesquasi{\langref{uOBDD}}{\langref{OBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{OBDD}}. If \langref{uOBDD} compiled to \langfam{OBDD}{<} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{uOBDD}}{\langref{OBDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{uOBDD}}{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{OBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{uOBDD}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{CNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{PI}}{\langref{CNF}}. If \langref{uOBDD} compiled to \langref{PI} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{CNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{uOBDD}}{\langref{PI}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langref{uOBDD}}. 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langref{SDD}}. If \langref{uOBDD} compiled to \langref{SDD} with polynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langref{SDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilespoly{\langref{uOBDD}}{\langref{SDD}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{uOBDD}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{OBDD}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{SDD}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. If \langref{uOBDD} compiled to \langfam{SDD}{T} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{OBDD}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{uOBDD}}{\langfam{SDD}{T}}.
\compilespoly{\langref{uOBDD}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-SDNNF}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langref{uOBDD}}{\langref{SDNNF}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{uOBDD}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{OBDD}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. If \langref{uOBDD} compiled to \langfam{SDNNF}{T} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{OBDD}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{uOBDD}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}.
As defined by \citet{Amarilli_2018}, \langref{uOBDD} is the subset of \langref{uFBDD} satisfying an order of the variables, so compilation entails discarding the order.

The hidden weighted bit function $HWB_n$ has polynomial-size \langref{uOBDD} representations under a fixed order $<$: $HWB_n = \bigvee_{k=1}^n(E_k^n(x) \wedge x_k)$, where each branch for $k$ tests the variables in the same order to verify the exact Hamming weight and $x_k=1$, and the branches are disjoint. Thus the representation is unambiguous and has polynomial size, as in the 2-\langref{OBDD}/\langref{FBDD} constructions discussed by \citet{Wegener_2000}. Bryant's hidden-weighted-bit lower bound says every \langref{OBDD} formula for $HWB_n$, regardless of variable order $<$, has exponential size \citep{Bryant_1986,Wegener_2000}. Hence no quasipolynomial compilation to \langref{OBDD} is possible.

\compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{uOBDD}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{OBDD}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{TDD}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. If \langref{uOBDD} compiled to \langfam{TDD}{T} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{OBDD}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{uOBDD}}{\langfam{TDD}{T}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langref{uOBDD}}. 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langref{SDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{TDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. If \langref{uOBDD} compiled to \langref{TDD} with polynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langref{SDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilespoly{\langref{uOBDD}}{\langref{TDD}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{FBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{d-DNNF}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{d-DNNF}}.
\compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{uOBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{uOBDD}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}.

ocompilesquasi{\langref{OBDD}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. If \langref{OBDD} compiled to \langfam{d-SDNNF}{T} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict

As defined by \citet{Darwiche_2002}, \langref{OBDD} is a subset of \langref{FBDD}. Therefore a polynomial time compilation is trivial.

\compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{OBDD}}.

ocompilesquasi{\langref{OBDD}}{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}. \compilespoly{\langref{MODS}}{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}. If \langref{OBDD} compiled to \langref{MODS} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{OBDD}}{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}. Therefore

\compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{FBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{uFBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{nFBDD}}.

Suppose an order $<$ existed so that \langref{OBDD} compiled to \langfam{OBDD}{<}. Take the first $2n$ variables in that order, call the first half $x_1,\ldots,x_n$ and the second half $y_1,\ldots,y_n$, and consider $EQ_n(x,y)$. This function has linear-size \langref{OBDD}{s} under the interleaved order $x_1,y_1,\ldots,x_n,y_n$, but exponential-size \langfam{OBDD}{<} representations \citep{Wegener_2000}. Thus no order $<$ can exist, and \langref{OBDD} is not quasipolynomial-time compilable to \langfam{OBDD}{<}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{OBDD}}.

ocompilesquasi{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{PI}}.

For any fixed vtree $T$, a simple function can be chosen so that every \langfam{SDNNF}{T} representation is exponentially large, while some \langref{OBDD} represents it in linear size. Therefore \langref{OBDD} cannot compile to \langfam{SDNNF}{T} in quasipolynomial size \citep{TCZ_Forthcoming}.
\compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{FBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{uFBDD}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{uFBDD}}.
As defined by \citet{Amarilli_2018}, \langref{OBDD} is the subset of \langref{uOBDD} without any $\vee$-node.

ocompilesquasi{\langref{OBDD}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{TDD}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. If \langref{OBDD} compiled to \langfam{TDD}{T} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict

ocompilesquasi{\langref{OBDD}}{\langfam{TDD}{T}}.

\citet[Theorem 17]{Capelli_2026} convert every fixed-order \langfam{OBDD}{<} into an equivalent \langfam{TDD}{T} of linear size for the corresponding linear vtree. Taking the union over orders gives a polynomial compilation from \langref{OBDD} to \langref{TDD}.
\compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{FBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{dec-DNNF}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{dec-DNNF}}.

Construct a right-linear vtree from the variable order, then rewrite each decision node labeled $x$ as $(x \wedge D_0) \vee (\neg x \wedge D_1)$, where $D_0, D_1$ are the rewritings of the 0- and 1-successors. The vtree alignment ensures structuredness, and since all $\vee$-gates arise from decision node rewrites, every $\vee$-gate is a decision gate, yielding a \langref{dec-SDNNF} in linear time \citep{Amarilli_2018}.

ocompilesquasi{\langref{OBDD}}{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}. \compilesquasi{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}. If \langref{OBDD} compiled to \langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict

ocompilesquasi{\langref{OBDD}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langfam{SDD}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{SDD}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{TDD}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-SDNNF}}{\langref{d-DNNF}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langfam{TDD}{T}}{\langref{d-DNNF}}.
\compilespoly{\langfam{TDD}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langfam{TDD}{T}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langfam{TDD}{T}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langfam{TDD}{T}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{DNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{IP}}{\langref{DNF}}. If \langfam{TDD}{T} compiled to \langref{IP} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict

\compilespoly{\langfam{TDD}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-SDNNF}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. \compilesquasi{\langref{SDNNF}}{\langref{nOBDD}}. Therefore \compilesquasi{\langfam{TDD}{T}}{\langref{nOBDD}}.

\citet[Theorem 21]{Capelli_2026} show that, given a \langfam{TDD}{T} $C$ respecting a vtree $T$, one can construct a vtree $T'$ and an equivalent \langfam{SDD}{T'} $C'$ with $|C'| = O(|C|^2)$. This gives a polynomial compilation from \langfam{TDD}{T} to the corresponding fixed-vtree family \langfam{SDD}{T'}. Under our definition the output vtree $T'$ need not match the input vtree $T$.

\compilespoly{\langfam{TDD}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-SDNNF}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langfam{TDD}{T}}{\langref{SDNNF}}.
\compilespoly{\langfam{TDD}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langfam{TDD}{T}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}.

\citet[Theorem 14]{Capelli_2026} give polynomial-size \langfam{TDD}{T} representations for bounded-treewidth instances over the vtrees used in the construction. Combined with Razgon's \langref{OBDD} lower bound for bounded-treewidth CNFs, as observed by \citet{Capelli_2026}, this rules out polynomial compilation to \langref{OBDD}. The paper explicitly leaves quasipolynomial simulation by \langref{OBDD}{s} open.
\langref{TDD} is the union of the fixed-vtree languages \langfam{TDD}{T}. Hence any \langfam{TDD}{T} representation is already a \langref{TDD} representation. \citep{Capelli_2026}

\compilespoly{\langref{TDD}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-SDNNF}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. \compilesquasi{\langref{SDNNF}}{\langref{nOBDD}}. Therefore \compilesquasi{\langref{TDD}}{\langref{nOBDD}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{TDD}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{OBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{TDD}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{CNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{PI}}{\langref{CNF}}. If \langref{TDD} compiled to \langref{PI} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{CNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{TDD}}{\langref{PI}}.

\citet[Theorem 21]{Capelli_2026} show that, given a \langref{TDD} $C$ respecting a vtree $T$, one can construct a vtree $T'$ and an equivalent \langfam{SDD}{T'} $C'$ with $|C'| = O(|C|^2)$. Thus every \langref{TDD} can be converted into an equivalent polynomial-size \langref{SDD}.

\compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{TDD}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{OBDD}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{SDD}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. If \langref{TDD} compiled to \langfam{SDD}{T} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{OBDD}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{TDD}}{\langfam{SDD}{T}}.
\compilespoly{\langref{TDD}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-SDNNF}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langref{TDD}}{\langref{SDNNF}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{TDD}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{OBDD}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. If \langref{TDD} compiled to \langfam{SDNNF}{T} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{OBDD}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{TDD}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{TDD}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilesquasi{\langref{d-SDNNF}}{\langref{uOBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{uOBDD}}{\langref{uFBDD}}. Therefore \compilesquasi{\langref{TDD}}{\langref{uFBDD}}.
\compilespoly{\langref{TDD}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilesquasi{\langref{d-SDNNF}}{\langref{uOBDD}}. Therefore \compilesquasi{\langref{TDD}}{\langref{uOBDD}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{TDD}{T}}{\langref{TDD}}. 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{TDD}{T}}{\langref{OBDD}}. If \langref{TDD} compiled to \langref{OBDD} with polynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{TDD}{T}}{\langref{OBDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilespoly{\langref{TDD}}{\langref{OBDD}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{TDD}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{OBDD}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{TDD}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. If \langref{TDD} compiled to \langfam{TDD}{T} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{OBDD}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{TDD}}{\langfam{TDD}{T}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{TDD}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{OBDD}}{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}. \compilesquasi{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}. If \langref{TDD} compiled to \langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{OBDD}}{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{TDD}}{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{TDD}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{OBDD}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langfam{SDD}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{SDD}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. If \langref{TDD} compiled to \langfam{cSDD}{T} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{OBDD}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{TDD}}{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{TDD}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{OBDD}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. If \langref{TDD} compiled to \langfam{nOBDD}{<} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{OBDD}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{TDD}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{TDD}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{OBDD}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. If \langref{TDD} compiled to \langfam{uOBDD}{<} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{OBDD}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{TDD}}{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{OBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{FBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{dec-DNNF}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{CNF}}. If \langref{dec-DNNF} compiled to \langref{CNF} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{CNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{dec-DNNF}}{\langref{CNF}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{dec-DNNF}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{cSDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. If \langref{dec-DNNF} compiled to \langref{cSDD} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{dec-DNNF}}{\langref{cSDD}}.
As defined by \citet{Amarilli_2018}, \langref{dec-DNNF} is the subset of \langref{d-DNNF} satisfying the decision property.

\compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{dec-DNNF}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-SDNNF}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. If \langref{dec-DNNF} compiled to \langref{d-SDNNF} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{dec-DNNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{dec-DNNF}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-SDNNF}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. If \langref{dec-DNNF} compiled to \langfam{d-SDNNF}{T} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{dec-DNNF}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{OBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{FBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{dec-DNNF}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{DNF}}. If \langref{dec-DNNF} compiled to \langref{DNF} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{DNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{dec-DNNF}}{\langref{DNF}}.
\compilespoly{\langref{dec-DNNF}}{\langref{d-DNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-DNNF}}{\langref{DNNF}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langref{dec-DNNF}}{\langref{DNNF}}.

noPolyDescription.description:
\compilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}{\langref{dec-DNNF}}. 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{uFBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. If \langref{dec-DNNF} compiled to \langref{FBDD} with polynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilespoly{\langref{dec-DNNF}}{\langref{FBDD}}.

matrix.description:
First, we show no polynomial compilation exists.
\compilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}{\langref{dec-DNNF}}. \nocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{uFBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. If \langref{dec-DNNF} compiled to \langref{FBDD} with polynomial blowup, this would contradict \nocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. Therefore \nocompilespoly{\langref{dec-DNNF}}{\langref{FBDD}}.

Now, we show a quasipolynomial compilation exists.
\citet[Corollary 3.2]{Beame_2013} shows quasipolynomial compilation from \langref{dec-DNNF} to \langref{FBDD}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{OBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{FBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{dec-DNNF}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{DNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{IP}}{\langref{DNF}}. If \langref{dec-DNNF} compiled to \langref{IP} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{DNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{dec-DNNF}}{\langref{IP}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{dec-DNNF}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{OBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{MODS}}{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{OBDD}}. If \langref{dec-DNNF} compiled to \langref{MODS} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{OBDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{dec-DNNF}}{\langref{MODS}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}{\langref{dec-DNNF}}. 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. If \langref{dec-DNNF} compiled to \langref{nFBDD} with polynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilespoly{\langref{dec-DNNF}}{\langref{nFBDD}}.

matrix.description:
First, we show no polynomial compilation exists.
\compilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}{\langref{dec-DNNF}}. \nocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. If \langref{dec-DNNF} compiled to \langref{nFBDD} with polynomial blowup, this would contradict \nocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. Therefore \nocompilespoly{\langref{dec-DNNF}}{\langref{nFBDD}}.

Now, we show a quasipolynomial compilation exists.
\compilespoly{\langref{dec-DNNF}}{\langref{d-DNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-DNNF}}{\langref{DNNF}}. \compilesquasi{\langref{DNNF}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. Therefore \compilesquasi{\langref{dec-DNNF}}{\langref{nFBDD}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{dec-DNNF}}{\langref{d-DNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-DNNF}}{\langref{DNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{DNNF}}{\langref{NNF}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langref{dec-DNNF}}{\langref{NNF}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{dec-DNNF}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{nOBDD}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. If \langref{dec-DNNF} compiled to \langref{nOBDD} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{dec-DNNF}}{\langref{nOBDD}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{dec-DNNF}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{OBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{OBDD}}. If \langref{dec-DNNF} compiled to \langfam{OBDD}{<} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{OBDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{dec-DNNF}}{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{OBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{FBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{dec-DNNF}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{CNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{PI}}{\langref{CNF}}. If \langref{dec-DNNF} compiled to \langref{PI} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{CNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{dec-DNNF}}{\langref{PI}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{dec-DNNF}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. If \langref{dec-DNNF} compiled to \langref{SDD} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{dec-DNNF}}{\langref{SDD}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{dec-DNNF}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{SDD}{T}}{\langref{SDD}}. If \langref{dec-DNNF} compiled to \langfam{SDD}{T} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{dec-DNNF}}{\langfam{SDD}{T}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{dec-DNNF}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. If \langref{dec-DNNF} compiled to \langref{SDNNF} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{dec-DNNF}}{\langref{SDNNF}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{dec-DNNF}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. If \langref{dec-DNNF} compiled to \langfam{SDNNF}{T} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{dec-DNNF}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}{\langref{dec-DNNF}}. 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. If \langref{dec-DNNF} compiled to \langref{uFBDD} with polynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilespoly{\langref{dec-DNNF}}{\langref{uFBDD}}.

matrix.description:
First, we show no polynomial compilation exists.
\compilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}{\langref{dec-DNNF}}. \nocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. If \langref{dec-DNNF} compiled to \langref{uFBDD} with polynomial blowup, this would contradict \nocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. Therefore \nocompilespoly{\langref{dec-DNNF}}{\langref{uFBDD}}.

Now, we show a quasipolynomial compilation exists.
\compilesquasi{\langref{dec-DNNF}}{\langref{FBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{uFBDD}}. Therefore \compilesquasi{\langref{dec-DNNF}}{\langref{uFBDD}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{dec-DNNF}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{uOBDD}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-SDNNF}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. If \langref{dec-DNNF} compiled to \langref{uOBDD} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{dec-DNNF}}{\langref{uOBDD}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{dec-DNNF}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{OBDD}}. If \langref{dec-DNNF} compiled to \langref{OBDD} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{OBDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{dec-DNNF}}{\langref{OBDD}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{dec-DNNF}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{TDD}{T}}{\langfam{SDD}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{SDD}{T}}{\langref{SDD}}. If \langref{dec-DNNF} compiled to \langfam{TDD}{T} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{dec-DNNF}}{\langfam{TDD}{T}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{dec-DNNF}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{TDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. If \langref{dec-DNNF} compiled to \langref{TDD} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{dec-DNNF}}{\langref{TDD}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{dec-DNNF}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{OBDD}}. \compilesquasi{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}{\langref{OBDD}}. If \langref{dec-DNNF} compiled to \langref{dec-SDNNF} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{OBDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{dec-DNNF}}{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{dec-DNNF}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{OBDD}}. \compilesquasi{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{OBDD}}. If \langref{dec-DNNF} compiled to \langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{OBDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{dec-DNNF}}{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{dec-DNNF}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langref{cSDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{cSDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. If \langref{dec-DNNF} compiled to \langfam{cSDD}{T} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{dec-DNNF}}{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{dec-DNNF}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langref{nOBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{nOBDD}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. If \langref{dec-DNNF} compiled to \langfam{nOBDD}{<} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{dec-DNNF}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{dec-DNNF}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-SDNNF}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. If \langref{dec-DNNF} compiled to \langfam{uOBDD}{<} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{dec-DNNF}}{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{OBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{CNF}}. If \langref{dec-SDNNF} compiled to \langref{CNF} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{CNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}{\langref{CNF}}.
\compilesquasi{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}{\langref{OBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{cSDD}}. Therefore \compilesquasi{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}{\langref{cSDD}}.
\compilespoly{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-SDNNF}}{\langref{d-DNNF}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}{\langref{d-DNNF}}.
As defined by \citet{Amarilli_2018}, \langref{dec-SDNNF} is the subset of \langref{d-SDNNF} satisfying the decision property, so compilation is trivial.

\compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{OBDD}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. If \langref{dec-SDNNF} compiled to \langfam{d-SDNNF}{T} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{OBDD}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{OBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{DNF}}. If \langref{dec-SDNNF} compiled to \langref{DNF} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{DNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}{\langref{DNF}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-SDNNF}}{\langref{d-DNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-DNNF}}{\langref{DNNF}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}{\langref{DNNF}}.

ocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{uFBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. If \langref{dec-SDNNF} compiled to \langref{FBDD} with polynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. Therefore

First, we show no polynomial compilation exists.
\compilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}. \nocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{uFBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. If \langref{dec-SDNNF} compiled to \langref{FBDD} with polynomial blowup, this would contradict \nocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. Therefore \nocompilespoly{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}{\langref{FBDD}}.

Now, we show a quasipolynomial compilation exists.
\compilesquasi{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}{\langref{OBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{FBDD}}. Therefore \compilesquasi{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}{\langref{FBDD}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{OBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}.

ocompilesquasi{\langref{OBDD}}{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}. Therefore

\compilesquasi{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}{\langref{OBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{cSDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{cSDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. Therefore \compilesquasi{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}{\langref{SDD}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}. 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{FBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{uFBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. If \langref{dec-SDNNF} compiled to \langref{OBDD} with polynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilespoly{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}{\langref{OBDD}}.

quasiDescription.description: \citet[Theorem 2]{Bollig_2018} shows that \langref{SDNNF} can be compiled into \langref{nOBDD} by adapting the compilation of \citet{Beame_2015} from \langref{DNNF} to \langref{nFBDD}; The same compilation yields an \langref{OBDD} if the input is a \langref{dec-SDNNF} since there are no $\vee$-gates that are not decision gates in a \langref{dec-SDNNF}.

matrix.description:
First, we show no polynomial compilation exists.
\compilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}. \nocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{FBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{uFBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. If \langref{dec-SDNNF} compiled to \langref{OBDD} with polynomial blowup, this would contradict \nocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. Therefore \nocompilespoly{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}{\langref{OBDD}}.

Now, we show a quasipolynomial compilation exists.
\citet[Theorem 2]{Bollig_2018} shows that \langref{SDNNF} can be compiled into \langref{nOBDD} by adapting the compilation of \citet{Beame_2015} from \langref{DNNF} to \langref{nFBDD}; The same compilation yields an \langref{OBDD} if the input is a \langref{dec-SDNNF} since there are no $\vee$-gates that are not decision gates in a \langref{dec-SDNNF}.

\compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{OBDD}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{TDD}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. If \langref{dec-SDNNF} compiled to \langfam{TDD}{T} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{OBDD}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}{\langfam{TDD}{T}}.
\compilesquasi{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}{\langref{OBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{TDD}}. Therefore \compilesquasi{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}{\langref{TDD}}.
As defined by \citet{Amarilli_2018}, a \langref{dec-SDNNF} is simply a structured \langref{dec-DNNF}, so discarding the vtree completes the compilation.

\compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{OBDD}}{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}. \compilesquasi{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}. If \langref{dec-SDNNF} compiled to \langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{OBDD}}{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{OBDD}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langfam{SDD}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{SDD}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. If \langref{dec-SDNNF} compiled to \langfam{cSDD}{T} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{OBDD}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{OBDD}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. If \langref{dec-SDNNF} compiled to \langfam{nOBDD}{<} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{OBDD}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{OBDD}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. If \langref{dec-SDNNF} compiled to \langfam{uOBDD}{<} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{OBDD}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{CNF}}. If \langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T} compiled to \langref{CNF} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{CNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{CNF}}.

\compilesquasi{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{OBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{cSDD}}. Therefore \compilesquasi{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{cSDD}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-SDNNF}}{\langref{d-DNNF}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{d-DNNF}}.
\compilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}.
For any fixed vtree $T$, \langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T} is the subset of \langfam{d-SDNNF}{T} satisfying the decision property, so compilation is trivial. \citep{Amarilli_2018}

\compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{DNF}}. If \langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T} compiled to \langref{DNF} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{DNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{DNF}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-SDNNF}}{\langref{d-DNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-DNNF}}{\langref{DNNF}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{DNNF}}.

noPolyDescription.description:
ocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{uFBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. If \langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T} compiled to \langref{FBDD} with polynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{FBDD}}.

\nocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{uFBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{DNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{IP}}{\langref{DNF}}. If \langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T} compiled to \langref{IP} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{DNF}}. Therefore

\compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{CNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{MODS}}{\langref{CNF}}. If \langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T} compiled to \langref{MODS} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{CNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{MODS}}.

noPolyDescription.description: This is the fixed-vtree form of Proposition 3.10 of \citet{Amarilli_2018}.

matrix.description:
First, we show no polynomial compilation exists.
This is the fixed-vtree form of Proposition 3.10 of \citet{Amarilli_2018}.

Now, we show a quasipolynomial compilation exists.
\compilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-SDNNF}}{\langref{d-DNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-DNNF}}{\langref{DNNF}}. \compilesquasi{\langref{DNNF}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. Therefore \compilesquasi{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-SDNNF}}{\langref{d-DNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-DNNF}}{\langref{DNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{DNNF}}{\langref{NNF}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{NNF}}.

ocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. Therefore
