First, we show no polynomial compilation exists.
\nocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{nOBDD}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. If \langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T} compiled to \langref{nOBDD} with polynomial blowup, this would contradict \nocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. Therefore \nocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nOBDD}}.

Now, we show a quasipolynomial compilation exists.
\compilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-SDNNF}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. \compilesquasi{\langref{SDNNF}}{\langref{nOBDD}}. Therefore \compilesquasi{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nOBDD}}.

noPolyDescription.description:
ocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{OBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{FBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{uFBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. If \langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T} compiled to \langfam{OBDD}{<} with polynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}.

quasiDescription.description: The common-order quasipolynomial simulation of \citet{TCZ_Forthcoming} specializes to \langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}: for every fixed source vtree $T$, it chooses one output order $<$ depending only on $T$ and produces a quasipolynomial-size \langfam{OBDD}{<}.

matrix.description:
First, we show no polynomial compilation exists.
\nocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{OBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{FBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{uFBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. If \langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T} compiled to \langfam{OBDD}{<} with polynomial blowup, this would contradict \nocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. Therefore \nocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}.

Now, we show a quasipolynomial compilation exists.
The common-order quasipolynomial simulation of \citet{TCZ_Forthcoming} specializes to \langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}: for every fixed source vtree $T$, it chooses one output order $<$ depending only on $T$ and produces a quasipolynomial-size \langfam{OBDD}{<}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{CNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{PI}}{\langref{CNF}}. If \langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T} compiled to \langref{PI} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{CNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{PI}}.

\compilesquasi{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{OBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{cSDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{cSDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. Therefore \compilesquasi{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{SDD}}.

\compilesquasi{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langfam{TDD}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{TDD}{T}}{\langfam{SDD}{T}}. Therefore \compilesquasi{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{SDD}{T}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-SDNNF}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{SDNNF}}.
\compilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}.

noPolyDescription.description:
ocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. If \langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T} compiled to \langref{uFBDD} with polynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{uFBDD}}.

matrix.description:
First, we show no polynomial compilation exists.
\nocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. If \langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T} compiled to \langref{uFBDD} with polynomial blowup, this would contradict \nocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. Therefore \nocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{uFBDD}}.

Now, we show a quasipolynomial compilation exists.
\compilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilesquasi{\langref{d-SDNNF}}{\langref{uOBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{uOBDD}}{\langref{uFBDD}}. Therefore \compilesquasi{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{uFBDD}}.

noPolyDescription.description:
ocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{uOBDD}}{\langref{nOBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{nOBDD}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. If \langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T} compiled to \langref{uOBDD} with polynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{uOBDD}}.

matrix.description:
First, we show no polynomial compilation exists.
\nocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{uOBDD}}{\langref{nOBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{nOBDD}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. If \langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T} compiled to \langref{uOBDD} with polynomial blowup, this would contradict \nocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. Therefore \nocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{uOBDD}}.

Now, we show a quasipolynomial compilation exists.
\compilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilesquasi{\langref{d-SDNNF}}{\langref{uOBDD}}. Therefore \compilesquasi{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{uOBDD}}.

noPolyDescription.description:
ocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{FBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{uFBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. If \langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T} compiled to \langref{OBDD} with polynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{OBDD}}.

matrix.description:
First, we show no polynomial compilation exists.
\nocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{FBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{FBDD}}{\langref{uFBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. If \langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T} compiled to \langref{OBDD} with polynomial blowup, this would contradict \nocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. Therefore \nocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{OBDD}}.

Now, we show a quasipolynomial compilation exists.
\compilesquasi{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{OBDD}}. Therefore \compilesquasi{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{OBDD}}.
\compilesquasi{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langfam{TDD}{T}}. Therefore \compilesquasi{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{TDD}{T}}.

\compilesquasi{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{OBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{TDD}}. Therefore \compilesquasi{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{TDD}}.
\compilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}{\langref{dec-DNNF}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{dec-DNNF}}.

\langref{dec-SDNNF} is the union of fixed-vtree \langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T} languages. Hence any fixed-class formula is already a \langref{dec-SDNNF} formula, so the compilation is trivial and polynomial-time. \citep{Amarilli_2018}
\compilesquasi{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}. Therefore \compilesquasi{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}.

noPolyDescription.description:
ocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langref{nOBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{nOBDD}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. If \langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T} compiled to \langfam{nOBDD}{<} with polynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}.

matrix.description:
First, we show no polynomial compilation exists.
\nocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langref{nOBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{nOBDD}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. If \langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T} compiled to \langfam{nOBDD}{<} with polynomial blowup, this would contradict \nocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. Therefore \nocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}.

Now, we show a quasipolynomial compilation exists.
\compilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. \compilesquasi{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}. Therefore \compilesquasi{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}.

noPolyDescription.description:
ocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langref{uOBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{uOBDD}}{\langref{nOBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{nOBDD}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. If \langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T} compiled to \langfam{uOBDD}{<} with polynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}.

matrix.description:
First, we show no polynomial compilation exists.
\nocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langref{uOBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{uOBDD}}{\langref{nOBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{nOBDD}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. If \langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T} compiled to \langfam{uOBDD}{<} with polynomial blowup, this would contradict \nocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. Therefore \nocompilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}.

Now, we show a quasipolynomial compilation exists.
\compilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilesquasi{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}. Therefore \compilesquasi{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{CNF}}. If \langfam{cSDD}{T} compiled to \langref{CNF} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{CNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langref{CNF}}.

\langref{cSDD} is the union of the fixed-vtree languages \langfam{cSDD}{T}. Hence any \langfam{cSDD}{T} formula is already a \langref{cSDD} formula, so the compilation is trivial and polynomial-time. \citep{Darwiche_2011}

\compilespoly{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langref{cSDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{cSDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{SDD}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-SDNNF}}{\langref{d-DNNF}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langref{d-DNNF}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langref{cSDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{cSDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{SDD}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}.
\compilespoly{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langfam{SDD}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{SDD}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{DNF}}. If \langfam{cSDD}{T} compiled to \langref{DNF} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{DNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langref{DNF}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langref{cSDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{cSDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{SDD}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-SDNNF}}{\langref{d-DNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-DNNF}}{\langref{DNNF}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langref{DNNF}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{DNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{IP}}{\langref{DNF}}. If \langfam{cSDD}{T} compiled to \langref{IP} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{DNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langref{IP}}.

ocompilesquasi{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}. \compilespoly{\langref{MODS}}{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}. If \langfam{cSDD}{T} compiled to \langref{MODS} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langref{MODS}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langref{cSDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{cSDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{SDD}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-SDNNF}}{\langref{d-DNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-DNNF}}{\langref{DNNF}}. \compilesquasi{\langref{DNNF}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. Therefore \compilesquasi{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langref{nFBDD}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langref{cSDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{cSDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{SDD}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-SDNNF}}{\langref{d-DNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-DNNF}}{\langref{DNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{DNNF}}{\langref{NNF}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langref{NNF}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langref{cSDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{cSDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{SDD}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-SDNNF}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. \compilesquasi{\langref{SDNNF}}{\langref{nOBDD}}. Therefore \compilesquasi{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langref{nOBDD}}.

\citet{Bova_2016_a} gives functions $F_n$ with polynomial-size \langfam{cSDD}{T} representations over explicit vtrees. Conditioning the auxiliary variables of $F_n$ recovers $HWB_n$, and conditioning a \langfam{OBDD}{<} preserves the order and cannot increase size. Since \citet{Bryant_1986,Wegener_2000} give exponential \langfam{OBDD}{<} size for $HWB_n$ for every order, every \langfam{OBDD}{<} for $F_n$ is exponential. Hence \langfam{cSDD}{T} is not quasipolynomial-time compilable to \langfam{OBDD}{<}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{CNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{PI}}{\langref{CNF}}. If \langfam{cSDD}{T} compiled to \langref{PI} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{CNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langref{PI}}.
\compilespoly{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langref{cSDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{cSDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langref{SDD}}.
For any fixed vtree $T$, \langfam{cSDD}{T} is the compressed subset of \langfam{SDD}{T}, so compilation is trivial. \citep{VanDenBroeck_2015}

\compilespoly{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langref{cSDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{cSDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{SDD}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-SDNNF}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langref{SDNNF}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langfam{SDD}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{SDD}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langref{cSDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{cSDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{SDD}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilesquasi{\langref{d-SDNNF}}{\langref{uOBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{uOBDD}}{\langref{uFBDD}}. Therefore \compilesquasi{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langref{uFBDD}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langref{cSDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{cSDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{SDD}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilesquasi{\langref{d-SDNNF}}{\langref{uOBDD}}. Therefore \compilesquasi{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langref{uOBDD}}.

\citet[Theorem 4]{Bova_2016_a} constructs a generalized hidden weighted bit function $F_n$ with polynomial-size \langfam{cSDD}{T} representations respecting explicit vtrees, but every \langref{OBDD} for $F_n$ has size $2^{\Omega(n)}$. Hence \langfam{cSDD}{T} is not quasipolynomial-time compilable to \langref{OBDD}.

ocompilesquasi{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langref{OBDD}}. \compilesquasi{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}{\langref{OBDD}}. If \langfam{cSDD}{T} compiled to \langref{dec-SDNNF} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langref{OBDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}.

ocompilesquasi{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}. \compilesquasi{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}. If \langfam{cSDD}{T} compiled to \langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langfam{SDD}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{SDD}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. \compilesquasi{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}. Therefore \compilesquasi{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langfam{SDD}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{SDD}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilesquasi{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}. Therefore \compilesquasi{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{IP}}{\langref{DNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{DNF}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{IP}}{\langref{CNF}}. If \langfam{nOBDD}{<} compiled to \langref{CNF} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{IP}}{\langref{CNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langref{CNF}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{DNF}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{cSDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{SDD}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. If \langfam{nOBDD}{<} compiled to \langref{cSDD} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langref{cSDD}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{DNF}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}. 
ocompilespoly{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{d-DNNF}}. If \langfam{nOBDD}{<} compiled to \langref{d-DNNF} with polynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilespoly{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{d-DNNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langref{d-DNNF}}.
matrix.assumption: the polynomial hierarchy does not collapse

matrix.description:
\compilespoly{\langref{DNF}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. If \langfam{nOBDD}{<} compiled to \langref{d-SDNNF} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{DNF}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. If \langfam{nOBDD}{<} compiled to \langfam{d-SDNNF}{T} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{DNF}}. If \langfam{nOBDD}{<} compiled to \langref{DNF} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{DNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langref{DNF}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langref{nOBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{nOBDD}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{nFBDD}}{\langref{DNNF}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langref{DNNF}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{IP}}{\langref{DNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{DNF}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{IP}}{\langref{FBDD}}. If \langfam{nOBDD}{<} compiled to \langref{FBDD} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{IP}}{\langref{FBDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langref{FBDD}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{DNF}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{IP}}. If \langfam{nOBDD}{<} compiled to \langref{IP} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{IP}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langref{IP}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{DNF}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{IP}}. \compilespoly{\langref{MODS}}{\langref{IP}}. If \langfam{nOBDD}{<} compiled to \langref{MODS} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{IP}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langref{MODS}}.
\compilespoly{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langref{nOBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{nOBDD}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langref{nFBDD}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langref{nOBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{nOBDD}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{nFBDD}}{\langref{DNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{DNNF}}{\langref{NNF}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langref{NNF}}.

\langref{nOBDD} is the union of fixed-order languages \langfam{nOBDD}{<}. Hence any \langfam{nOBDD}{<} formula is already an \langref{nOBDD} formula, so the compilation is trivial and polynomial-time. \citep{Wegener_2000}

\compilespoly{\langref{DNF}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{OBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{OBDD}}. If \langfam{nOBDD}{<} compiled to \langfam{OBDD}{<} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{OBDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{IP}}{\langref{DNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{DNF}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{IP}}{\langref{CNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{PI}}{\langref{CNF}}. If \langfam{nOBDD}{<} compiled to \langref{PI} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{IP}}{\langref{CNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langref{PI}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{DNF}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{SDD}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. If \langfam{nOBDD}{<} compiled to \langref{SDD} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langref{SDD}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{DNF}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{SDD}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. If \langfam{nOBDD}{<} compiled to \langfam{SDD}{T} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langfam{SDD}{T}}.
\compilespoly{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langref{nOBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{nOBDD}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langref{SDNNF}}.

Fix any variable order $<$ and let $T$ be the corresponding right-linear vtree. Rewriting each decision node labeled $x$ as $(x \wedge D_0) \vee (\neg x \wedge D_1)$ yields an output respecting $T$, so \langfam{nOBDD}{<} compiles to \langfam{SDNNF}{T} in linear time. \citep{Amarilli_2018}

\compilespoly{\langref{DNF}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}. 
ocompilespoly{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{d-DNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{uFBDD}}{\langref{d-DNNF}}. If \langfam{nOBDD}{<} compiled to \langref{uFBDD} with polynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilespoly{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{d-DNNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langref{uFBDD}}.
matrix.assumption: the polynomial hierarchy does not collapse

matrix.description:
\compilespoly{\langref{DNF}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{uOBDD}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. If \langfam{nOBDD}{<} compiled to \langref{uOBDD} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langref{uOBDD}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{DNF}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{OBDD}}. If \langfam{nOBDD}{<} compiled to \langref{OBDD} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{OBDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langref{OBDD}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{DNF}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{TDD}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. If \langfam{nOBDD}{<} compiled to \langfam{TDD}{T} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langfam{TDD}{T}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{DNF}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{TDD}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. If \langfam{nOBDD}{<} compiled to \langref{TDD} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langref{TDD}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{IP}}{\langref{DNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{DNF}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{IP}}{\langref{FBDD}}. \compilesquasi{\langref{dec-DNNF}}{\langref{FBDD}}. If \langfam{nOBDD}{<} compiled to \langref{dec-DNNF} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{IP}}{\langref{FBDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langref{dec-DNNF}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{DNF}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. If \langfam{nOBDD}{<} compiled to \langref{dec-SDNNF} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{DNF}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. If \langfam{nOBDD}{<} compiled to \langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{DNF}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langref{cSDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{cSDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{SDD}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. If \langfam{nOBDD}{<} compiled to \langfam{cSDD}{T} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}.

\compilespoly{\langref{DNF}}{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. If \langfam{nOBDD}{<} compiled to \langfam{uOBDD}{<} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langref{DNF}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{nOBDD}{<}}{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{CNF}}. If \langfam{uOBDD}{<} compiled to \langref{CNF} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{CNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langref{CNF}}.

ocompilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langref{SDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{cSDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. If \langfam{uOBDD}{<} compiled to \langref{cSDD} with polynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langref{SDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langref{cSDD}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-SDNNF}}{\langref{d-DNNF}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langref{d-DNNF}}.
\compilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}.

Fix any variable order $<$ and let $T$ be the corresponding right-linear vtree. Rewriting each decision node labeled $x$ as $(x \wedge D_0) \vee (\neg x \wedge D_1)$ yields a structured output respecting $T$, and unambiguity ensures determinism of introduced $\vee$-gates, giving \langfam{d-SDNNF}{T} in linear time. \citep{Amarilli_2018}

\compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{DNF}}. If \langfam{uOBDD}{<} compiled to \langref{DNF} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{DNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langref{DNF}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-SDNNF}}{\langref{d-DNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-DNNF}}{\langref{DNNF}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langref{DNNF}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{SDD}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilesquasi{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{SDD}{T}}{\langref{FBDD}}. If \langfam{uOBDD}{<} compiled to \langref{FBDD} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{SDD}{T}}{\langref{FBDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langref{FBDD}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{DNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{IP}}{\langref{DNF}}. If \langfam{uOBDD}{<} compiled to \langref{IP} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{DNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langref{IP}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{CNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{MODS}}{\langref{CNF}}. If \langfam{uOBDD}{<} compiled to \langref{MODS} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{CNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langref{MODS}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langref{uOBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{uOBDD}}{\langref{nOBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{nOBDD}}{\langref{nFBDD}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langref{nFBDD}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-SDNNF}}{\langref{d-DNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-DNNF}}{\langref{DNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{DNNF}}{\langref{NNF}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langref{NNF}}.
\compilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langref{uOBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{uOBDD}}{\langref{nOBDD}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langref{nOBDD}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langfam{SDD}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{SDD}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilesquasi{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}. If \langfam{uOBDD}{<} compiled to \langfam{OBDD}{<} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{CNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{PI}}{\langref{CNF}}. If \langfam{uOBDD}{<} compiled to \langref{PI} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}{\langref{CNF}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langref{PI}}.

The proof of \citet[Theorem 2]{Vinall-Smeeth_2024} constructs unambiguous DNFs $f$ whose negation requires quasipolynomially larger SDNNFs. One may convert an unambiguous DNF to an equivalent \langfam{uOBDD}{<} with only a linear change in size (for any order $<$). However, any \langref{SDD} computing $f$ may be efficiently negated (\supportspoly{\langref{SDD}}{\NOTC}) and then efficiently converted to an equivalent \langref{SDNNF}. A quasipolynomial separation in the \langfam{uOBDD}{<} and \langref{SDD} sizes of $f$ follows. \citep{Darwiche_2011}

ocompilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langref{SDD}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{SDD}{T}}{\langref{SDD}}. If \langfam{uOBDD}{<} compiled to \langfam{SDD}{T} with polynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langref{SDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langfam{SDD}{T}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langref{d-SDNNF}}. \compilespoly{\langref{d-SDNNF}}{\langref{SDNNF}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langref{SDNNF}}.
\compilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langfam{SDNNF}{T}}.
\compilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langref{uOBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{uOBDD}}{\langref{uFBDD}}. Therefore \compilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langref{uFBDD}}.

\langref{uOBDD} is the union of fixed-order languages \langfam{uOBDD}{<}. Hence any \langfam{uOBDD}{<} formula is already a \langref{uOBDD} formula, so the compilation is trivial and polynomial-time. \citep{Wegener_2000}

\compilespoly{\langfam{SDD}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilesquasi{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{SDD}{T}}{\langref{FBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{FBDD}}. If \langfam{uOBDD}{<} compiled to \langref{OBDD} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{SDD}{T}}{\langref{FBDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langref{OBDD}}.

ocompilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langref{SDD}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{TDD}{T}}{\langfam{SDD}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{SDD}{T}}{\langref{SDD}}. If \langfam{uOBDD}{<} compiled to \langfam{TDD}{T} with polynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langref{SDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langfam{TDD}{T}}.

ocompilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langref{SDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{TDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. If \langfam{uOBDD}{<} compiled to \langref{TDD} with polynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langref{SDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langref{TDD}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{SDD}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilesquasi{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{SDD}{T}}{\langref{FBDD}}. \compilesquasi{\langref{dec-DNNF}}{\langref{FBDD}}. If \langfam{uOBDD}{<} compiled to \langref{dec-DNNF} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{SDD}{T}}{\langref{FBDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langref{dec-DNNF}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{SDD}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilesquasi{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{SDD}{T}}{\langref{FBDD}}. \compilesquasi{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}{\langref{OBDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{OBDD}}{\langref{FBDD}}. If \langfam{uOBDD}{<} compiled to \langref{dec-SDNNF} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{SDD}{T}}{\langref{FBDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langref{dec-SDNNF}}.

\compilespoly{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langfam{SDD}{T}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{SDD}{T}}{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}. \compilesquasi{\langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}. 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}. \compilesquasi{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}. If \langfam{uOBDD}{<} compiled to \langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T} with quasipolynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langfam{OBDD}{<}}. Therefore 
ocompilesquasi{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T}}.

ocompilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langref{SDD}}. \compilespoly{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}{\langref{cSDD}}. \compilespoly{\langref{cSDD}}{\langref{SDD}}. If \langfam{uOBDD}{<} compiled to \langfam{cSDD}{T} with polynomial blowup, this would contradict 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langref{SDD}}. Therefore 
ocompilespoly{\langfam{uOBDD}{<}}{\langfam{cSDD}{T}}.

For any fixed variable order $<$, every \langfam{uOBDD}{<} formula is a \langfam{nOBDD}{<} formula respecting the same order $<$. Thus, for each $<$, \langfam{uOBDD}{<} $\subset$ \langfam{nOBDD}{<}, and the compilation is the identity. \citep{Amarilli_2018,Wegener_2000}

definitions.title: Representation Language
definitions.statement: A \emph{representation language} $L$ is a set of Boolean formulas sharing a common syntactic structure, endowed with the standard Boolean semantics and a size measure $|\cdot|$, usually simply the count of all variables and connectives in the formula. \citep{Darwiche_2002}

definitions.title: Language Class
Succinctness Between Languages
definitions.statement:
There are two intuitive definitions of succinctness: a \emph{time} requirement, i.e. that an efficient compilation algorithm exists between two languages; or the \emph{space} requirement, that formulas remain ``small'' in the new language. Since, for any computation, runtime is lower-bounded by output size, the latter definition is weaker.

We fix the convention that succinctness between representation languages is a size-only relation, with no requirement that a conversion algorithm exist \citep{Gogic_1995}. However, to the best of our knowledge, all known proofs of succinctness use a compilation algorithm to establish the size relationship; and all proofs that \emph{no} time-efficient compilation algorithm exists use space complexity bounds. An example to the contrary would be highly interesting.

Formally, let $L_1$ and $L_2$ be two languages. $L_1$ is \emph{at least as succinct as} $L_2$, written $L_1 \leq_p L_2$, if there exists a polynomial $p$ such that for every $\alpha \in L_2$ there exists an equivalent $\beta \in L_1$ with $|\beta| \leq p(|\alpha|)$.

We say $L_1$ \emph{polynomially compiles into} $L_2$ if there exists a polynomial-time algorithm that converts any $\alpha \in L_1$ into an equivalent $\beta \in L_2$.

Since all claims in this project work with either definition, we will sometimes use them as if they were interchangeable.

We additionally define quasipolynomial succinctness identically, except we require a quasipolynomial function $q(n) \in n^{log^{O(1)}(n)}$ satisfying the above properties and use the notation $\leq_q$. \citep{Darwiche_2002}

definitions.title: Succinctness Between Classes
Tractability of an Operation
definitions.statement: An operation $\mathcal{O}$ is \emph{tractable} on a representation language $L$ if there exists a polynomial-time (resp. quasipolynomial-time) algorithm that performs $\mathcal{O}$ on any input formula(s) in $L$. Following \citet{Selman_1996}, the point is to compile a propositional theory off-line into a language where target operations are tractable. \citep{Darwiche_2002}

definitions.title: Query
definitions.statement:
A \emph{query} takes a formula $\alpha \in L$ (and possibly auxiliary inputs) and returns a Boolean answer or a numerical value without modifying $\alpha$.

The standard query operations in this project are Consistency, Validity, Clausal Entailment, Implicant, Equivalence, Model Counting, Model Enumeration, and Sentential Entailment. \citep{Darwiche_2002}

definitions.title: Transformation
definitions.statement:
A \emph{transformation} maps one or more formulas from $\alpha_1,\alpha_2,..., \alpha_m\ \in L$, where $m$ may be arbitrarily large, to a new formula $\alpha_{m+1} \in L$.

The standard transformations in this project are Conditioning, Forgetting, Singleton Forgetting, Conjunction, Bounded Conjunction, Disjunction, Bounded Disjunction, and Negation. \citep{Darwiche_2002}

definitions.title: Consistency (CO)
definitions.statement: Given a representation $\alpha$, decide whether $\alpha$ is satisfiable, i.e., whether there exists an assignment $X$ such that $\alpha(X)=\top$. \citep{Darwiche_2002}

definitions.title: Validity (VA)
definitions.statement: Given a representation $\alpha$, decide whether $\alpha$ is valid, i.e., $\alpha \equiv \top$. \citep{Darwiche_2002}

definitions.title: Clausal Entailment (CE)
definitions.statement: Given a representation $\alpha$ and a clause $c$, decide whether $\alpha \models c$ (equivalently, whether $\alpha \wedge \neg c$ is unsatisfiable). \citep{Darwiche_2002}

definitions.title: Implicant (IM)
definitions.statement: Given a representation $\alpha$ and a term $t$, decide whether $t \models \alpha$ (equivalently, whether $t \wedge \neg \alpha$ is unsatisfiable). \citep{Darwiche_2002}

definitions.title: Equivalence (EQ)
definitions.statement: Given two representations $\alpha$ and $\beta$, decide whether $\alpha \equiv \beta$. \citep{Darwiche_2002}

definitions.title: Model Counting (CT)
definitions.statement: Given a representation $\alpha$, compute the number of satisfying assignments: $|\{X:\alpha(X)=\top\}|$. \citep{Darwiche_2002}

definitions.title: Model Enumeration (ME)
definitions.statement: Given a representation $\alpha$, enumerate all satisfying assignments, i.e., output $\{X:\alpha(X)=\top\}$, in time polynomial in the input size and in the total size of the output. \citep{Darwiche_2002}

definitions.title: Sentential Entailment (SE)
definitions.statement: Given representations $\alpha$ and $\beta$, decide whether $\alpha \models \beta$. \citep{Darwiche_2002}

definitions.title: Conditioning (CD)
definitions.statement: Given a representation $\alpha$ and a consistent term $\gamma$, compute the conditioned representation $\alpha|_{\gamma}$ obtained by substituting the assignments in $\gamma$. \citep{Darwiche_2002}

definitions.title: Forgetting (FO)
definitions.statement: Given a representation $\alpha$ and a set of variables $Y$, compute $\exists Y.\alpha$ (existentially quantifying the variables in $Y$). \citep{Darwiche_2002}

definitions.title: Singleton Forgetting (SFO)
definitions.statement: Given a representation $\alpha$ and a variable $x$, compute $\exists x.\alpha = (\alpha|_{x=0}) \vee (\alpha|_{x=1})$. \citep{Darwiche_2002}

definitions.title: Conjunction ($\wedge$C)
definitions.statement: Given representations $\alpha_1,\ldots,\alpha_m$, compute $\bigwedge_{i=1}^m \alpha_i$. \citep{Darwiche_2002}

definitions.title: Bounded Conjunction ($\wedge$BC)
definitions.statement: Given two representations $\alpha_1,\alpha_2$, compute $\alpha_1 \wedge \alpha_2$. \citep{Darwiche_2002}

definitions.title: Disjunction ($\vee$C)
definitions.statement: Given representations $\alpha_1,\ldots,\alpha_m$, compute $\bigvee_{i=1}^m \alpha_i$. \citep{Darwiche_2002}

definitions.title: Bounded Disjunction ($\vee$BC)
definitions.statement: Given two representations $\alpha_1,\alpha_2$, compute $\alpha_1 \vee \alpha_2$. \citep{Darwiche_2002}

definitions.title: Negation ($\neg$C)
definitions.statement: Given a representation $\alpha$, compute $\neg \alpha$. \citep{Darwiche_2002}

operationLemmas.description: CO and CD imply ME: First test consistency (polytime via CO). If inconsistent, return the empty set. Otherwise, build a decision tree of models by iterating over variables $x_1, \ldots, x_n$ and using CD to test consistency of $\Sigma | \alpha \land x_i$ and $\Sigma | \alpha \land \neg x_i$ for each leaf term $\alpha$. Since one branch must succeed for consistent terms, the tree has $O(mn)$ tests where $m$ is the model count and $n$ is the variable count.
CO and CD imply CE: To test whether representation $\Sigma$ entails non-valid clause $\alpha$, i.e. $\Sigma \models \alpha$, it suffices to test whether $\Sigma | \neg\alpha$ is inconsistent.

VA and CD imply IM: To test whether consistent term $\alpha$ entails representation $\Sigma$, i.e. $\alpha \models \Sigma$, test whether $
eg\alpha \lor \Sigma$ is valid. This is equivalent to $
eg\alpha \lor (\Sigma | \alpha)$. Since $
eg\alpha$ and $\Sigma | \alpha$ share no variables, the disjunction is valid iff $
eg\alpha$ is valid or $\Sigma | \alpha$ is valid. Since $\alpha$ is consistent, $
eg\alpha$ cannot be valid. Thus we test validity of $\Sigma | \alpha$, constructible in polytime via CD and testable in polytime via VA.
EQ implies CO: $\mathsf{true}$ and $\mathsf{false}$ belong to every NNF subset. $\Sigma$ is inconsistent iff it is equivalent to $\mathsf{false}$.
EQ implies VA: $\mathsf{true}$ and $\mathsf{false}$ belong to every NNF subset. $\Sigma$ is valid iff it is equivalent to $\mathsf{true}$.
SE implies EQ: Sentences $\Sigma_1$ and $\Sigma_2$ are equivalent iff $\Sigma_1 \models \Sigma_2$ and $\Sigma_2 \models \Sigma_1$.
CT implies CO: Model counting (CT) requires enumerating all satisfying assignments. If a formula is inconsistent, it has zero models. Thus CT can determine CO by checking if the model count is zero.
CT implies VA: A formula is valid iff all $2^n$ assignments satisfy it. If we can count models in polytime (CT), we can check validity by testing if the count equals $2^n$.

IM implies VA: A formula $\Sigma$ is valid iff the complete term (all positive or all negative literals, i.e., any single model) implies $\Sigma$. Since IM tests whether a consistent term implies the representation, testing with a trivially consistent term gives validity.
CE implies CO: A formula $\Sigma$ is inconsistent iff $\Sigma \models \bot$, which is equivalent to $\Sigma$ entailing the empty clause. Clause entailment (CE) can test this directly.

ME implies CO: Model Enumeration requires outputting all models in time polynomial in the input size and number of models. In particular, it must determine whether any model exists (output the first model or halt with the empty set) in polynomial time, which is exactly the Consistency (CO) query.

Conjunction implies bounded conjunction: bounded conjunction is a special case of conjunction where one operand has bounded size, so any language closed under conjunction is also closed under bounded conjunction.

Disjunction implies bounded disjunction: bounded disjunction is a special case of disjunction where one operand has bounded size, so any language closed under disjunction is also closed under bounded disjunction.
Forgetting implies singleton forgetting: singleton forgetting eliminates a single variable, which is a special case of forgetting an arbitrary set of variables.

Conditioning and bounded disjunction imply singleton forgetting: to forget variable $x$ from $\Sigma$, compute $\Sigma|_{x=0}$ and $\Sigma|_{x=1}$ via conditioning, then combine via $\Sigma|_{x=0} \lor \Sigma|_{x=1}$ using bounded disjunction (one operand can serve as the bounded component).
Negation and conjunction imply disjunction (De Morgan): $\Sigma_1 \lor \Sigma_2 = \lnot(\lnot\Sigma_1 \land \lnot\Sigma_2)$. Negate both operands, conjoin, then negate the result.
Negation and disjunction imply conjunction (De Morgan): $\Sigma_1 \land \Sigma_2 = \lnot(\lnot\Sigma_1 \lor \lnot\Sigma_2)$. Negate both operands, disjoin, then negate the result.

Negation and bounded conjunction imply bounded disjunction (De Morgan): $\Sigma \lor \gamma = \lnot(\lnot\Sigma \land \lnot\gamma)$, where $\lnot\gamma$ (negation of a clause) is a term. Negate $\Sigma$, conjoin with the term $\lnot\gamma$ via bounded conjunction, then negate.

Negation and bounded disjunction imply bounded conjunction (De Morgan): $\Sigma \land \alpha = \lnot(\lnot\Sigma \lor \lnot\alpha)$, where $\lnot\alpha$ (negation of a term) is a clause. Negate $\Sigma$, disjoin with the clause $\lnot\alpha$ via bounded disjunction, then negate.
Negation and consistency imply validity: $\Sigma$ is valid iff $\lnot\Sigma$ is inconsistent. Negate via $\lnot$C, then test consistency via CO.
Negation and validity imply consistency: $\Sigma$ is consistent iff $\lnot\Sigma$ is not valid. Negate via $\lnot$C, then test validity via VA.

Negation and clausal entailment imply implicant testing: term $\alpha$ implies $\Sigma$ iff $\lnot\Sigma$ entails clause $\lnot\alpha$. Negate $\Sigma$ via $\lnot$C, then test clausal entailment of $\lnot\alpha$ (a clause) via CE.

Negation and implicant testing imply clausal entailment: $\Sigma$ entails clause $\gamma$ iff term $\lnot\gamma$ implies $\lnot\Sigma$. Negate $\Sigma$ via $\lnot$C, then test whether $\lnot\gamma$ (a term) implies $\lnot\Sigma$ via IM.

Negation, bounded conjunction, and consistency imply sentential entailment: $\Sigma_1 \models \Sigma_2$ iff $\Sigma_1 \land \lnot\Sigma_2$ is unsatisfiable. Negate $\Sigma_2$ via $\lnot$C, conjoin with $\Sigma_1$ via $\land$BC, then test consistency via CO.

Bounded conjunction and singleton forgetting imply conditioning: $\Sigma|\gamma$ for term $\gamma = l_1 \land \cdots \land l_k$ is computed iteratively by $\Sigma \leftarrow \exists x_i.(\Sigma \land l_i)$ for each literal $l_i$, using $\land$BC then SFO.

CT and $\land$BC imply SE: $\Sigma_1 \models \Sigma_2$ iff $|\text{models}(\Sigma_1 \land \Sigma_2)| = |\text{models}(\Sigma_1)|$. Compute the conjunction via $\land$BC, then count models of both the original formula and the conjunction via CT and compare.

assumptions: the polynomial hierarchy does not collapse
